bull.b: Sustained positive operating and free cash flow (~$330M OCF, ~$293M FCF) with high conversion to earnings (~0.89) provides durable internal funding for brand investment, integration costs, buybacks, and debt service. Achieving roughly 20.9% dollar share in core U.S. energy signals durable category leadership and scale. Multi-brand positioning (Celsius, Alani, Rockstar) and expanded retail distribution create shelf presence and bargaining power with retailers, underpinning sustained revenue growth potential and resilience versus sin

About $50M of integration synergies and a sharp adjusted EBITDA margin expansion (~370bps to 24.9%) demonstrate tangible operating leverage from acquisitions. Realized cost savings, SKU optimization and SG&A efficiencies are structural benefits that can sustain higher profitability as Rockstar integration completes and

bear.b: Material increase in debt (~$2.44B) and lower equity reduce financial flexibility versus prior low-leverage profile. Higher leverage raises interest and refinancing sensitivity, constrains capacity for opportunistic M&A or marketing spend, and amplifies risk if commodity or demand shocks delay margin recovery.

bear.t: Compressed Net Margins Versus Prior Years
bear.b: Net margin compression to ~5% despite stable gross margins reflects lower-margin acquired brands, integration costs and elevated operating expenses. An official state AG probe into marketing practices and youth appeal of Alani Nu creates structural compliance, labeling and litigation risk.
